statusBanner.allMapped: All {{count}} categories are connected to Swiss law
statusBanner.allMappedDesc: When a tenant submits a maintenance request, the legal engine will automatically look up depreciation standards and rent reduction rules for every category.

statusBanner.unmapped_other: {{unmapped}} of {{total}} categories aren’t connected to Swiss law yet
statusBanner.unmappedDesc: The legal engine can’t find depreciation standards or rent reduction rules for unmapped categories. Scroll down to set them up.

howItWorks.title: What does a mapping do?
howItWorks.description: Each mapping connects a maintenance category (what the tenant sees) to a legal topic (what the engine searches). When a tenant reports an issue — say, a broken dishwasher — the engine uses the mapping to find two things:
howItWorks.deprecDesc: How old is the item and what’s its expected lifespan? This determines who pays for repairs — the tenant or the landlord.
howItWorks.rentDesc: Is the tenant entitled to a rent reduction while the issue isn’t fixed? Swiss law defines specific percentages per defect type.

card.notConnected: Not connected — the engine can’t look up legal references for this category yet.
card.resetTitle: Remove your override and use the system default
card.editTitle: Change the legal topic for this category
card.activeTitle: Active — the engine uses this mapping. Click to disable.
card.inactiveTitle: Disabled — the engine skips this mapping. Click to re-enable.
card.changeHint: Change what the engine searches when a tenant reports a “{{label}}” issue:
card.checkHint: When a tenant reports a {{label}} issue, the engine checks:

deprecBox.title: Depreciation
deprecBox.whoPaysSub: → Determines who pays: landlord (if past lifespan) or shared cost
deprecBox.noData: No depreciation data for this topic. Cost-sharing can’t be calculated automatically.

rentBox.title: Rent Reduction
rentBox.claimSub: → Tells you if the tenant can claim a rent reduction while unfixed
rentBox.noRules: No specific rent reduction rules. The tenant can’t auto-claim a reduction for this type.

text.selectBuilding: Select a building in the NOI panel above to see its capex schedule.
text.noAssetsHint: Add assets with installation dates to generate a replacement forecast.
text.chartNote: Derived from asset depreciation timelines. Darker bar = tax-deductible portion.
text.missingDateHint: set installation date in asset inventory
text.nearingEolTitle: Nearing end of useful life — beyond {{toYear}}
text.nearingEolHint: These assets are ≥60% depreciated but not due within the projection horizon. Plan ahead.

scenario.neglectHint: No ongoing capex — NOI erodes after grace period; backlog charged at horizon end or at sale

text.selectBuilding: Select a building in the NOI panel above.
text.footnote: Discount rate {{discount}}% · Income growth {{growth}}% · Standard DCF, cash flows at end of each year.
text.terminalValueMissing: Terminal value impairment not modeled — enter est. property value above to include sale price impact
text.taxShieldDefaultNote: default {{rate}}% — set owner rate in settings

tooltip.npv: Net Present Value — the sum of all future cash flows (NOI minus capex plus tax shield) discounted back to today's value.
tooltip.noi: Net Operating Income — rental income minus operating expenses, projected forward with the income growth rate.
tooltip.taxShield: Deductible (value-preserving) maintenance reduces your taxable income in the year it is expensed. This is the cumulative benefit over the horizon.
tooltip.fci: Facility Condition Index = deferred maintenance cost ÷ total replacement value. BOMA benchmarks: <5% excellent, 5–10% good, 10–30% fair, >30% critical.
tooltip.discountRate: Annualised cost of capital (opportunity cost). Swiss private real estate benchmark: 3–5%. Higher rates penalise distant cash flows more.
tooltip.incomeGrowth: Expected annual rental income growth rate. Swiss residential long-run average: 1–2%. Inflation + market tightness drive this.
tooltip.deferWindow: Number of years by which near-term capex is postponed in the Defer scenario. Assets due beyond this window are still executed on schedule.
tooltip.equityNpv: Value created in today’s money after paying the mortgage. Positive = the plan adds wealth.
tooltip.equityIrr: The yearly % return on the cash you actually put in, accounting for the loan.
tooltip.dscr: Rent vs loan payments. 1.2× means rent covers payments 1.2 times — above ~1.2× is healthy.
tooltip.ltv: Share of the building’s value funded by debt. Lower = safer; banks often cap around 80%.
tooltip.wacc: Your blended cost of money across equity and debt — the hurdle a plan must beat.
tooltip.costOfDebt: The average interest rate across your mortgages.
tooltip.equity: Your own money in the building: market value minus debt.

notice.identicalScenarios: No capex falls in the first {{years}} years of this building's schedule — Invest and Defer are equivalent under current assumptions. Try a shorter defer window to see a difference.

summary.invest: Executing capex on schedule outperforms neglect by {{delta}} over the projection horizon. Maintains asset condition on the optimal schedule.
summary.investNeutral: Investing on schedule maintains asset condition. No significant NPV difference vs neglect under current assumptions.
summary.investDeferBetter: Deferring {{years}} years produces a higher NPV by {{diff}}. Executing on-schedule still protects condition but costs more in present-value terms.
summary.defer: Deferring {{years}} years costs {{diff}} in NPV vs investing on schedule. Reduces near-term cash outflow but delays value protection.
summary.deferWins: Deferring {{years}} years outperforms on-schedule investment by {{diff}} in NPV. Near-term capex is more expensive in present-value terms than waiting.
summary.neglect: The best approach outperforms neglect by {{delta}}. NOI erosion and end-of-horizon backlog compound over time.
summary.neglectNeutral: No significant NPV advantage over neglect under current assumptions — review income and capex estimates.

delta.investWins: Investing on schedule outperforms neglect by {{delta}} over {{years}} years.
delta.deferWins: Deferring {{deferYears}} years is the strongest approach, outperforming neglect by {{delta}} over {{years}} years.
delta.neglectWins: Neglect produces a higher NPV by {{delta}} over {{years}} years — review capex estimates and income assumptions.

debt.title: Leverage & equity returns
debt.noData: Add a market value and mortgage(s) under Financing to see leveraged returns.

plain.verdictInvest: Best move: renovate now — it adds about {{delta}} in today’s money versus doing nothing.
plain.verdictDefer: Best move: wait about {{years}} years — deferring is worth roughly {{delta}} more than acting now.
plain.verdictNeglect: Best move right now: hold off — spending on this over this period wouldn’t pay for itself.
plain.coverageComfortable: The building comfortably covers its loan payments ({{dscr}}× rent-to-debt, above the 1.2× comfort line).
plain.coverageOk: The building covers its loan payments ({{dscr}}× rent-to-debt).
plain.coverageTight: Watch the loan coverage — {{dscr}}× rent-to-debt is below the 1.2× comfort line.
plain.noDebt: No mortgage on file, so this is an all-cash view.
plain.returnNote: Your money back at about {{irr}}% per year on the cash invested.
